laziness.name: the Dragon of Laziness
laziness.reflect: Laziness whispers “later.” You chose {{activities}} — what does the version of you who acts now gain that “tomorrow you” keeps losing?
laziness.reflectWhy: Laziness feeds on delay. One started rep starves it.

fear.name: the Dragon of Fear
fear.reflect: Fear asks “what if you fail?” Picture finishing {{activities}} today — what’s the worst that truly happens, and who do you become by doing it scared?
fear.reflectWhy: Courage isn’t the absence of fear — it’s acting before you feel ready.

unworthiness.name: the Dragon of Unworthiness
unworthiness.victory: Unworthiness slain — you showed up for yourself.
unworthiness.reflect: Unworthiness says you’re not enough. What would doing {{activities}} prove to that doubting voice — and whose voice is it, really?
unworthiness.reflectWhy: Worth is built by showing up, not by waiting to feel worthy.

tooold.name: the Dragon of “Too Old”
tooold.reflect: “Too late” is the lie. If age weren’t a factor, how would you attack {{activities}} — and what’s one rep that makes that real today?
tooold.reflectWhy: The best day was yesterday; the next best is today.

procrastination.name: the Dragon of Procrastination
procrastination.reflect: Procrastination loves “in a minute.” What’s the smallest first move on {{activities}} you can’t talk yourself out of — and what will trigger it?
procrastination.reflectWhy: Starting is the whole battle — motion makes momentum.

doubt.name: the Dragon of Doubt
doubt.reflect: Doubt asks “will this even work?” What past evidence says you can do {{activities}} — and what proof will you add today?
doubt.reflectWhy: Proof beats opinion. Each rep is evidence.

custom.name: your Dragon
custom.reflect: Name your dragon. What’s really stopping you from {{activities}} — and what truth is stronger than the excuse?
